paper.title_en: First measurement of B(E3;31−→01+) in $^{90}$Zr through Coulomb excitation: Revisiting octupole collectivity in the Zr chain
paper.abstract_en: The 31− state at an excitation energy of 2748 keV in the N=50 semi-magic 90Zr nucleus was investigated via safe Coulomb excitation in the 32S+90Zr system. The octupole transition strength, B(E3;31−→01+), was extracted by fitting the measured γ-ray intensities using the semiclassical least-squares search codes gosia and gosia2. A pronounced E3 strength of 19.6−1.6+1.7 W.u. was obtained, indicating a collective octupole character for this state. State-of-the-art shell model calculations reproduce this result by describing the excitation as emergent collectivity rooted in microscopic configuration mixing, whereas pure harmonic vibrational models tend to overestimate the strength. In addition, the spectroscopic quadrupole moment of the 21+ state in 90Zr was determined to be +0.09(10) eb, by normalizing the target excitation to the Coulomb-excitation intensities of the 32S projectile in a simultaneous analysis. This value is consistent with the near-spherical structure expected for a semi-magic nucleus. The results yield a coherent picture of collectivity in 90Zr, and are discussed in the context of the evolution of E3 strength along the stable Zr chain towards the N=56 octupole-magic neutron number.
